title: WindowsNativeJail crate LICENSE AUDIT + adoption decision (codex-windows-sandbox vs rappct)
goal: Perform the mandatory license + maturity audit of the two candidate Rust crates flagged by RW-1 (`codex-windows-sandbox` and `rappct`) BEFORE WindowsNativeJailAdapter (MT-046) adopts either one. Per refinement R-10 ('WindowsNativeJailAdapter-crate-license') a license audit MT is required prior to crate adoption. This MT produces a decision-record artifact under .GOV that captures license terms, repository activity, security posture, dependency footprint, and the chosen crate; MT-046 reads the decision and depends on it.
expected_diff_shape: New decision-record artifact at .GOV/roles_shared/records/sandbox/WP-KERNEL-004-windows-native-jail-crate-license-audit.json (machine-readable per RGF-324, NO md sibling). New audit script .GOV/roles_shared/scripts/sandbox-crate-license-audit.mjs that fetches each candidate's Cargo.toml + LICENSE + repo metadata and emits the decision record. No Rust src changes in this MT (MT-046 consumes the decision record).
unblock_conditions: None; runs concurrent with MT-041/MT-042/MT-043/MT-044 since it only produces a decision record.
implementation_notes: Audit criteria (each candidate gets a row): (1) license SPDX identifier (MIT/Apache-2.0/MPL-2.0/GPL-3.0/Proprietary/Unclear) read from the crate's LICENSE-* files AND crates.io metadata AND the Cargo.toml [package] license field — any disagreement is itself a finding; (2) repository activity: last commit date, open-issue count, maintainer count, presence of CI; (3) security posture: cargo-audit clean / advisories from rustsec.org against the crate's declared version; (4) dependency footprint: transitive dep count, presence of unmaintained-or-deprecated deps (yanked crates, deprecated bindings); (5) Win32 API surface coverage: does the crate wrap Job Objects + AppContainer + Restricted Tokens (spec §3.5.2 trinity) or only a subset?; (6) async surface: tokio-compatible vs blocking-only; (7) test coverage: presence of integration tests against actual Win32 isolation; (8) HARD blockers: GPL-3.0 (incompatible with Handshake's licensing posture per CX-503 family — TBD; flag for operator), unmaintained > 18 months (rejection criterion), open CVE in current version (rejection). Audit script: `node .GOV/roles_shared/scripts/sandbox-crate-license-audit.mjs --candidates codex-windows-sandbox,rappct --emit .GOV/roles_shared/records/sandbox/WP-KERNEL-004-windows-native-jail-crate-license-audit.json`. The script fetches crates.io API metadata (https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/<name>), parses the readme/license, walks the latest version's Cargo.toml, and runs `cargo tree` if a local checkout is present (optional). Decision record schema: `{ schema: "hsk.sandbox_crate_audit@1", audited_at_utc, audited_by: "KERNEL_BUILDER-MT-045", candidates: [{ crate_name, version_audited, license_spdx, license_findings: [...], last_commit_utc, open_issues, maintainer_count, ci_present, cargo_audit_clean, transitive_dep_count, unmaintained_deps: [...], win32_surface_coverage: { job_objects: bool, app_container: bool, restricted_tokens: bool }, async_compatible: bool, integration_tests_present: bool, hard_blockers: [...], score: 0..100 }], decision: { chosen_crate, chosen_version, rationale, alternative_rejected, alternative_rejected_reason, license_acceptance_path, operator_escalation_required: bool } }`. If BOTH candidates hit a hard blocker, decision.chosen_crate = null, decision.operator_escalation_required = true, and MT-046 is BLOCKED until operator resolves. If neither is GPL and both are MIT/Apache, prefer the higher-scoring one; tie-break by maintenance recency. The decision record is the authority — MT-046 reads only this file and never re-runs the audit.
risk_if_missed: Without the audit, MT-046 adopts a crate that may carry GPL-3.0 (forcing Handshake's product code into copyleft) or be unmaintained (silent supply-chain risk).
validator_focus: Decision record schema validates; both candidates were actually audited (not just one); hard_blockers field is populated explicitly (a `[]` is acceptable, missing field is not); decision.chosen_crate is either a candidate name OR null with operator_escalation_required==true.

minimum_controls: Decision record schema validation rejects a record with missing hard_blockers field (forces explicit empty array if no blockers).
GPL-3.0 license MUST appear in hard_blockers for any candidate carrying it; this is a kernel_builder discipline gate.
If decision.chosen_crate==null, MT-046 lifecycle.depends_on remains blocking until operator resolves (orchestrator surface, not silent skip).
